item.title: Citi Scraps Calls for RBI Hikes as Iran Deal Cools Price Risks
item.description: Economists at Citigroup Inc. dropped their call for two interest-rate hikes by India’s central bank through March next year, saying an interim US-Iran peace deal has reduced the risk of higher oil prices fueling inflation.

item.title: India Taps World Bank, ADB for $2.5 Billion Infrastructure Spend
item.description: India is said to be in talks with multilateral lenders to secure about $2.5 billion in funding from existing credit lines as New Delhi tees up fresh sources of money after the Middle East conflict crimped its ability to step up spending.

item.title: Kingboard’s 570% Rally Is Being Powered by Mainland Investors
item.description: Chinese investors have fueled a surge in shares of newfound AI darling Kingboard Laminates Holdings Ltd., more than doubling their stake in the company to 13% this year, according to the latest stock exchange data.

item.title: Copper Falls After Fed Chairman Warsh Positions as Inflation Hawk
item.description: Copper fell more than 1% to wipe out the gains of earlier this week after US Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh sparked speculation about rate hikes in comments at his debut press conference.

item.title: Mukesh Ambani’s Promise to Investors on Jio IPO Will Be Tested This Week
item.description: The long-running saga over the listing of Reliance Industries Ltd.’s digital unit comes to the fore this week, as investors watch whether its billionaire chairman Mukesh Ambani can walk the talk from last year.

item.title: Circles Sold Phone Spy Tools to Repressive States, Report Says
item.description: A Bulgaria-based company sold controversial surveillance technology to governments in countries with records of repression, enabling authorities to track mobile phones and eavesdrop on private communications, according to documents obtained by Human Rights Watch.

item.title: Europe Bets Industrial AI Can Salvage Its Manufacturing Edge
item.description: Pressure to become more efficient has Europe racing to bring artificial intelligence to the shop floor.

item.title: Czechs Mull First Rate Hike Since 2022 to Curb Price Pressures
item.description: The Czech central bank may raise interest rates for the first time in four years as policymakers weigh domestic inflation pressures against receding risks from global energy prices.

item.title: Europe Tries to Take On China Without Launching a New Trade War
item.description: The Group of Seven industrial nations this week pledged an ambitious target to diversify away from China, adding momentum to the European Union’s own push to counter a growing trade imbalance with the world’s biggest export engine.

item.title: Rio Tinto Resumes Copper Exports From Massive Mongolian Mine
item.description: Rio Tinto Group has resumed exports of copper concentrate from its vast Oyu Tolgoi copper mine in Mongolia following a short protest.

item.title: India Monsoon Starts Almost 40% Short as El Niño Upends Weather
item.description: India’s monsoon has had an unusually weak start and the lackluster rainfall is likely to persist, threatening crops and industrial activity as the El Niño weather phenomenon takes hold.

item.title: Goldman Says Hormuz Oil Flows May Recover to Only 70% After War
item.description: Oil flows going through the Strait of Hormuz may recover to only about 70% of their pre-war level, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., which highlighted regional producers leaning on alternative routes.

item.title: Brookfield Sells Wembley Stadium Builder Multiplex for $650 Million
item.description: Brookfield Business Corp. has agreed to sell Multiplex, the construction company that built London’s Wembley Stadium, to Japan’s Obayashi Corp. for $650 million.

item.title: PBOC’s New Rate Regime to Aid Liquidity and Bonds, Analysts Say
item.description: The People’s Bank of China’s latest refinement of its monetary policy toolkit is expected to damp money-market volatility and support bonds, according to analysts.

item.title: Morgan Stanley, Temasek Set for Big Pay Day From NSE’s India IPO
item.description: The National Stock Exchange of India Ltd.’s planned initial public offering is set to unlock the value of long-term investments for a group of backers including Morgan Stanley, Temasek Holdings Pte. and State Bank of India.

item.title: Korea Rejects Baemin, Coupang Settlement Bids in Antitrust Probe
item.description: South Korea’s antitrust watchdog has rejected settlement proposals from the country’s two largest food delivery platforms, Baedal Minjok and Coupang Eats, leaving both firms exposed to potentially significant fines over alleged unfair business practices.

item.title: Tencent-Backed Fintech Uzum Plans New Funding Round by Year-End
item.description: Uzbek fintech Uzum, backed by investors including China’s Tencent Holdings Ltd., aims to hold another funding round as the group seeks to support rapid expansion plans ahead of a potential initial public offering.

item.title: Star Google Researcher Joins OpenAI in Coup for ChatGPT Creator
item.description: One of Google’s most prominent researchers is leaving for rival OpenAI, dealing a setback to Alphabet Inc. in a multibillion-dollar race to build the world’s most powerful artificial intelligence models.

item.title: Lithium Bulls Eye Demand Support as China Heads For Busy Season
item.description: The global lithium market has taken a positive turn as analysts see demand from energy storage systems and electric vehicles spurring gains for the battery material.

item.title: Jera Books Mitsui OSK Ships to Bring Low-Carbon Ammonia to Japan
item.description: Jera Co. has agreed to book two gas carriers from Mitsui OSK Lines Ltd. that will enable Japan’s largest power producer to transport low-carbon ammonia from the US and build up a supply chain for the alternative fuel.

item.title: LME to Launch Steel Futures Giving Access to Shanghai Prices
item.description: The London Metal Exchange plans to launch a steel futures contract that gives global investors exposure to Shanghai prices, marking another step in the internationalization of Chinese commodities futures.

item.title: BOK Study Flags Gap Between Overseas Income and Currency Inflows
item.description: South Korea’s growing investment income from overseas assets may do less to support the won than expected, as a large share of those funds gets retained and reinvested abroad rather than repatriated, according to a Bank of Korea study.

item.title: Citi Analyst Blasted by Indonesia Says Fiscal Risks Have Peaked
item.description: Fiscal and currency risks have likely passed in Indonesia, according to a Citigroup Inc. analyst who was once lambasted by the country’s finance chief for his forecasts.

item.title: AI Startup Midjourney Pivots to Health With Ultrasound Machine
item.description: AI startup Midjourney Inc. announced its first hardware project at an event in San Francisco, outlining an unexpected move into the personal health and medical industries.

item.title: Pictet, Neuberger to Boost India Bond Exposure on Policy Support
item.description: Global funds are piling into Indian government bonds after New Delhi removed taxes on debt for foreign investors and eased ownership caps, along with moves that have helped stabilize the rupee.

item.title: NSE Filing Puts India IPO Market Back in Focus
item.description: NSE is currently valued at about $53 billion in the unlisted market.

item.title: What’s at Stake If Indonesia Loses Its Emerging Market Status
item.description: Indonesia has spent decades establishing itself as one of the world’s most important emerging markets. Now the country — Southeast Asia’s largest economy, with a gross domestic product of about $1.5 trillion — risks losing that standing, potentially jeopardizing billions of dollars in foreign investment.

item.title: China’s Tilt to Bonds From Loans Gives PBOC Broader Easing Tool
item.description: The increasingly large role of China’s bond market as a source of credit for the nation’s borrowers has offered the central bank an important channel for broad reductions in borrowing costs.

item.title: Lululemon Apologizes After Great Wall Drum Sparks China Backlash
item.description: Lululemon Athletica Inc. apologized after a yoga event staged on the Great Wall featuring a Japanese taiko drum sparked social media backlash in China, highlighting the reputational risks facing the company in its fastest-growing major market.

item.title: Goldman’s Kaplan Says Fed May Hike by Fall, Sees Risk of More
item.description: The Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates as soon as September if inflation remains elevated, according to Rob Kaplan, vice chairman at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and former Dallas Fed president.
